summary:
The foramen caecum is an embryonic remnant located at the base of the tongue, not within cranial bones like the ethmoid or sphenoid. It is commonly mistaken for structures related to the cecum, but these are anatomically distinct.  

\boxed{D} 

Note: Despite retrieval showing absence of direct mentions, established medical literature consistently places the foramen caecum on the tongue—supporting choice D when interpreted as referencing the correct anatomical site(s). Final decision remains based on accurate physiological knowledge rather than document matches alone.  

(End summary)
